title: The End of the Tunnel — Level 1 Training Detective
description: LEVEL 1 TRAINING DETECTIVE version of The End of the Tunnel with unique suspect, method, motive, evidence, and answers.

answerKey.method: The poisoned champagne flute switched during the Peacock Alley toast
answerKey.motive: To hide that she forged a Grosjean letter and sold it as authentic
answerKey.keyEvidence: lipstick on the wrong flute, stage cue card, and the torn auction receipt
answerKey.explanation: Celeste Moreau had access to Peacock Alley, a reason to bury the truth, and the timing to use the blackout. The proof path combines lipstick on the wrong flute, stage cue card, and the torn auction receipt with the failed statement that the tunnel access had not been used recently.

publicClues.title: Case Opened
publicClues.text: The End of the Tunnel begins at The Tunnel in Baton Rouge. Peacock Alley, the passage between the Heidelberg and King hotels, is back in the spotlight.

publicClues.title: Private Preview
publicClues.text: The victim arrived with a hotel ledger, a King Hotel key, and a sealed envelope marked DO NOT DELIVER.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 1 Available
publicClues.text: Round 1 is ready. Review the body scene and the blackout before answering.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 2 Available
publicClues.text: Round 2 is ready. Compare the Heidelberg and King Hotel timeline.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 3 Available
publicClues.text: Round 3 is ready. Keys, doors, and access logs now matter.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 4 Available
publicClues.text: Round 4 is ready. Separate the old rumor from the modern motive.

publicClues.title: Final Accusation Open
publicClues.text: Final questions are open. Complete all questions before the case locks.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: The victim missed two calls from Celeste Moreau before the lights went out.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: A final eleven-second call connected as the victim moved toward the King Hotel door.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: The victim texted: “The tunnel was opened recently. I can prove it.”

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: A reply from an unknown number said: “Not in the lobby. Bring the ledger below.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: The victim’s phone pinged near the Heidelberg service stairs at 8:07 PM.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: A second device appears near the sealed passage during the same blackout window.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 1
bank.text: A private payment references “Peacock Alley restoration materials” but posts to a personal account.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 2
bank.text: The payout trail points back to Celeste Moreau through a vendor alias.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 1
photos.text: A guest photo shows a chipped mosaic tile near the victim’s hand.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 2
photos.text: Another photo captures the tunnel rope moved before the official reveal.

social.title: Social Evidence 1
social.text: A teaser video about Huey Long rumors was posted minutes before the murder.

social.title: Social Evidence 2
social.text: The reposted clip cuts out the seconds showing someone cross behind the bar curtain.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 1
contacts.text: The victim saved Celeste Moreau under “Tunnel access follow-up.”

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: A second contact note says: “Ask about King Hotel key stamp.”

notes.title: Notes Evidence 1
notes.text: Detective note: the killer needed access, timing, and a reason to turn history into misdirection.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 2
notes.text: Detective note: the scene points to a planned cover-up, not an old curse or random accident.

files.title: Files Evidence 1
files.text: A security export skips the exact Peacock Alley blackout window.

files.title: Files Evidence 2
files.text: File metadata shows the export was edited after the preview guest list was finalized.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 1
browser.text: A browser search asks how to age paper and remove modern ink fluorescence.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 2
browser.text: Another search matches the cover story: “Huey Long tunnel mistress rumor Heidelberg King Hotel.”

rounds.title: Round 1: Peacock Alley Goes Dark
rounds.objective: Review the body scene, the blackout, and what the victim was holding at the tunnel door.
rounds.dialogue: The first truth is not in the rumor. It is in the room: who could reach the victim when Peacock Alley went dark?
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 1 is nearly complete. Lock in what the body scene proves before the next round opens.

rounds.title: Round 2: The Heidelberg Timeline
rounds.objective: Compare the movements between the Heidelberg side, the King Hotel side, and the speakeasy floor.
rounds.dialogue: The tunnel once solved problems for hotel guests. Tonight, it gives one suspect a false alibi.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 2 is nearly complete. Find who could move without being seen.

rounds.title: Round 3: The King Hotel Door
rounds.objective: Study keys, service panels, valet slips, and staff access around the sealed passage.
rounds.dialogue: A sealed door is only sealed to people without the right key. Follow the access evidence.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 3 is nearly complete. Decide which access clue breaks the story.

rounds.title: Round 4: The Rumor Machine
rounds.objective: Separate historic rumor from modern motive. The old legend is being used as cover.
rounds.dialogue: Huey Long, Peacock Alley, and the old hotel gossip are smoke. Someone created that smoke on purpose.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 4 is nearly complete. Identify what the killer was really hiding.

rounds.title: Round 5: The End of the Tunnel
rounds.objective: Use the final evidence to decide who used the tunnel, who staged the scene, and why.
rounds.dialogue: At the end of the tunnel is not a ghost story. It is a present-day crime with fingerprints.
rounds.countdownReview: The final window is opening. Prepare the accusation.

rounds.title: Final Accusation
rounds.objective: Answer the final five questions to close The End of the Tunnel.
rounds.dialogue: Final accusation is open. Name the killer, method, motive, evidence, and explanation.
rounds.countdownReview: The final window is closing. Submit all answers before The End of the Tunnel locks.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to The End of the Tunnel at The Tunnel in Baton Rouge. You are playing Level 1 Training Detective. Peacock Alley once connected the Heidelberg and King hotels. Tonight, a historian is dead, and an old political rumor is covering a modern crime.
revealScript: The old rumor was bait. Celeste Moreau used Peacock Alley as cover and killed to keep a present-day secret buried. The end of the tunnel was not the past. It was access, motive, and evidence pointing back to Celeste Moreau.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 1: What makes the death suspicious?

options.label: A normal guest tripped near the bar
The victim was found at a restricted tunnel door during a staged blackout
A menu had the wrong cocktail price
A jazz song ended early

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 2: Which timeline clue matters most?

options.label: The singer took a longer encore
The suspect could move between the Heidelberg side and King Hotel side during the blackout
A guest paid cash for a drink
The valet stand was busy

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 3: Which access clue breaks the case open?

options.label: A supposedly sealed service door shows recent use tied to the suspect
The public restroom line was long
A table candle burned low
The bartender changed playlists

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 4: What was the old Huey Long rumor doing in the case?

options.label: It proved the tunnel was haunted
It identified every hotel guest
It distracted everyone from a modern crime happening through the tunnel
It explained why the lights were decorative

questions.prompt: Final Accusation: Who killed the victim?

options.label: Celeste Moreau
Vivian Cross
Julian March
Celeste Moreau
Rhett Boudreaux
Nora Vale
Bennett Clay
Mara King

questions.prompt: Final Accusation: What method was used?

options.label: A random fall during the crowded toast
The poisoned champagne flute switched during the Peacock Alley toast
A fight outside the lobby
A fake ghost scare that went wrong

questions.prompt: Final Accusation: What was the motive?

options.label: To hide that she forged a Grosjean letter and sold it as authentic
To cancel jazz night
To steal an ordinary bar tab
To prove the tunnel was never real

questions.prompt: Final Accusation: Which evidence combination proves it?

options.label: A cocktail umbrella, a napkin, and a playlist
lipstick on the wrong flute, stage cue card, and the torn auction receipt
A parking ticket, a menu typo, and a receipt
A rumor, a song request, and a cracked glass

questions.prompt: Final Accusation: What is the real truth of the case?

options.label: The victim was killed over a confirmed historic romance
The historic rumor was used to hide a present-day crime inside the tunnel
No one had access to the passage
The murder was unrelated to the preview

questions.prompt: Final Accusation: Why does the title matter?

options.label: Because the killer’s alibi ends where the tunnel access evidence begins
Because the victim never entered the building
Because the tunnel only appears in a song lyric
Because the King Hotel key was decorative
